one_line: A long-distance trader of the Vijayanagara Empire linking Hampi’s bazaars to coastal ports and Persian Gulf horse markets.

appearance.face: oval face with sharp nose and strong jaw, medium-brown South Indian complexion, neatly groomed short beard and moustache, calm observant eyes with a confident half-smile as default expression
appearance.hair: thick black hair parted slightly off-center, combed back and tied into a small low knot at the nape, front kept smooth, with a plain off-white cotton headcloth casually wrapped once around the crown like a light turban and knotted at the back
appearance.costume: A mid-15th–16th century South Indian merchant’s outfit: a knee-length off-white handwoven cotton angarkha-style tunic with side tie and narrow V-neck, long sleeves rolled neatly to mid-forearm; a deep indigo-dyed cotton dhoti wrapped to ankle-length with a broad border in muted marigold yellow and brick-red stripes, pleats falling straight in front; a short sleeveless brown leather waistcoat over the tunic suggesting travel readiness; a simple cotton waistband in marigold yellow; on the feet, plain brown leather sandals; minimal jewellery—a thin gold chain, small gold stud earrings, and a few dark wooden prayer beads on his right wrist—kept subtle to signal mercantile sobriety while still prosperous.
appearance.signature: A rectangular dark teakwood merchant’s chest carried by a leather shoulder strap, its lid slightly open to reveal a neatly stacked small bag of gold varaha pagoda coins and a folded palm-leaf ledger inscribed with trade accounts.

ref_desc: A mid-30s Vijayanagara merchant with medium-brown oval face, black tied-back hair under an off-white headcloth, off-white tunic, indigo dhoti with marigold and brick-red border, brown leather vest and sandals, carrying a dark teakwood chest of gold varaha coins and a palm-leaf ledger.
sheet_prompt: Draw a full-body anime-style model sheet of a mid-30s Vijayanagara merchant: medium-brown South Indian complexion, oval face, sharp nose, short beard and moustache, calm observant eyes, black hair combed back into a low knot, wrapped once with a plain off-white cotton headcloth. He wears a knee-length off-white cotton tunic with a narrow V-neck and side tie, sleeves rolled to mid-forearm, over a deep indigo ankle-length dhoti with a broad marigold yellow and brick-red striped border, the front pleats falling straight. Over the tunic sits a short sleeveless brown leather waistcoat, secured at the front, with a marigold cotton waistband at his waist and simple brown leather sandals on his feet. Jewellery is minimal: thin gold chain, small gold ear studs, and dark wooden prayer beads on his right wrist. His signature prop is a rectangular dark teakwood merchant’s chest on a leather shoulder strap, shown clearly with its lid slightly open to reveal a small bag of gold varaha pagoda coins and a folded palm-leaf ledger inside. Use a palette of off-white, deep indigo, marigold yellow, brick red and dark brown, and present him front-facing in a neutral, confident stance so the costume and details are clear for consistent redrawing.
